prompt_preview:
You are a classical planning system. You are given a planning domain and a problem both in PDDL syntax.

STRICT REQUIREMENT: Your response must contain ONLY the executable plan — nothing else. No preamble, no explanation, no "We need to...", no natural-language commentary, no markdown unless it is a

message.content:
(detect_lost_checkpoint primary_role_1)
(detect_lost_checkpoint secondary_role_1)
(detect_lost_checkpoint coordinator_alpha)
(detect_lost_checkpoint coordinator_beta)
(assign_backup_resource primary_role_1 backup_token_a)
(assign_backup_resource secondary_role_1 backup_token_b)
(propose_loadout_for_recovery primary_role_1 loadout_option_alpha)
(commit_loadout_selection primary_role_1 loadout_option_alpha)
(attach_support_unit primary_role_1 support_unit_alpha)
(probe_route_segment_primary primary_role_1 route_segment_1 loadout_option_alpha)
(validate_primary_segment_traversal primary_role_1 route_segment_1 support_unit_alpha)
(detach_support_unit primary_role_1 support_unit_alpha)
(release_loadout_option primary_role_1 loadout_option_alpha)
(propose_loadout_for_recovery secondary_role_1 loadout_option_alpha)
(commit_loadout_selection secondary_role_1 loadout_option_alpha)
(attach_support_unit secondary_role_1 support_unit_alpha)
(probe_route_segment_secondary secondary_role_1 objective_node_1 loadout_option_alpha)
(validate_secondary_segment_traversal secondary_role_1 objective_node_1 support_unit_alpha)
(detach_support_unit secondary_role_1 support_unit_alpha)
(release_loadout_option secondary_role_1 loadout_option_alpha)
(assemble_checkpoint_bundle_standard primary_role_1 secondary_role_1 route_segment_1 objective_node_1 checkpoint_bundle_alpha)
(propose_loadout_for_recovery primary_role_1 loadout_option_alpha)
(validate_checkpoint_bundle checkpoint_bundle_alpha primary_role_1 loadout_option_alpha)
(release_loadout_option primary_role_1 loadout_option_alpha)
(mark_primary_ready primary_role_1 checkpoint_bundle_alpha)
(mark_secondary_ready secondary_role_1 checkpoint_bundle_alpha)
(assign_backup_resource coordinator_alpha backup_token_c)
(propose_loadout_for_recovery coordinator_alpha loadout_option_alpha)
(commit_loadout_selection coordinator_alpha loadout_option_alpha)
(integrate_progress_fragment coordinator_alpha progress_fragment_alpha checkpoint_bundle_alpha)
(finalize_fragment_integration coordinator_alpha progress_fragment_alpha checkpoint_bundle_alpha loadout_option_alpha)
(attach_equipment_module coordinator_alpha equipment_module_alpha)
(attach_support_unit coordinator_alpha support_unit_alpha)
(apply_equipment_module_phase1 coordinator_alpha equipment_module_alpha support_unit_alpha progress_fragment_alpha checkpoint_bundle_alpha)
(attach_mission_modifier coordinator_alpha mission_modifier_alpha)
(sequence_mission_modifier_step1 coordinator_alpha mission_modifier_alpha progress_fragment_alpha checkpoint_bundle_alpha)
(finalize_modifier_and_mark_ready coordinator_alpha)
(release_loadout_option coordinator_alpha loadout_option_alpha)
(detach_support_unit coordinator_alpha support_unit_alpha)
(detach_equipment_module coordinator_alpha equipment_module_alpha)
(detach_mission_modifier coordinator_alpha mission_modifier_alpha)
(propose_loadout_for_recovery primary_role_1 loadout_option_alpha)
(attach_failure_marker_to_participant primary_role_1 failure_marker_alpha loadout_option_alpha)
(bind_backup_to_primary_and_resume primary_role_1 backup_token_a failure_marker_alpha)
(release_loadout_option primary_role_1 loadout_option_alpha)
(assign_backup_resource coordinator_beta backup_token_a)
(propose_loadout_for_recovery coordinator_beta loadout_option_alpha)
(commit_loadout_selection coordinator_beta loadout_option_alpha)
(integrate_progress_fragment coordinator_beta progress_fragment_beta checkpoint_bundle_alpha)
(finalize_fragment_integration coordinator_beta progress_fragment_beta checkpoint_bundle_alpha loadout_option_alpha)
(attach_equipment_module coordinator_beta equipment_module_alpha)
(attach_support_unit coordinator_beta support_unit_alpha)
(apply_equipment_module_phase1 coordinator_beta equipment_module_alpha support_unit_alpha progress_fragment_beta checkpoint_bundle_alpha)
(attach_mission_modifier coordinator_beta mission_modifier_alpha)
(sequence_mission_modifier_step1 coordinator_beta mission_modifier_alpha progress_fragment_beta checkpoint_bundle_alpha)
(finalize_modifier_and_mark_ready coordinator_beta)
(release_loadout_option coordinator_beta loadout_option_alpha)
(propose_loadout_for_recovery secondary_role_1 loadout_option_alpha)
(attach_failure_marker_to_participant secondary_role_1 failure_marker_alpha loadout_option_alpha)
(bind_backup_to_secondary_and_resume secondary_role_1 backup_token_b failure_marker_alpha)
(release_loadout_option secondary_role_1 loadout_option_alpha)
(propose_loadout_for_recovery coordinator_alpha loadout_option_alpha)
(attach_failure_marker_to_participant coordinator_alpha failure_marker_alpha loadout_option_alpha)
(bind_backup_to_coordinator_and_resume coordinator_alpha backup_token_c failure_marker_alpha)
(release_loadout_option coordinator_alpha loadout_option_alpha)
(propose_loadout_for_recovery coordinator_beta loadout_option_alpha)
(attach_failure_marker_to_participant coordinator_beta failure_marker_alpha loadout_option_alpha)
(bind_backup_to_coordinator_and_resume coordinator_beta backup_token_a failure_marker_alpha)
message.reasoning:
The problem is a classical planning problem in PDDL for "lost_checkpoint_recovery". I need to output only the executable plan as a sequence of ground PDDL actions, one per line, in the format (action-name arg1 arg2 ...).


## Examining the Problem  
- The task involves solving a classical planning problem in PDDL for "lost_checkpoint_recovery."  
- The goal is to output an executable plan as a sequence of ground PDDL actions, one per line, in the format (action-name arg1 arg2 ...).

## Examining the Problem  
- The goal is to restore checkpoints for primary_role_1, secondary_role_1, coordinator_alpha, and coordinator_beta, all of which are participants in the recovery process.  
- Initial state includes available resources, roles linked to segments and nodes, and coordinators with primaries, secondaries, bundles, and fragments, but no lost checkpoint detected yet.  

## Identifying Recovery Steps  
- Recovery begins with detecting a lost checkpoint for each participant: primary_role_1, secondary_role_1, coordinator_alpha, and coordinator_beta.  
- The process involves assigning a backup resource, proposing and committing a loadout, and attaching supports, with final actions like bind_backup_to_primary_and_resume to restore checkpoints.  

## Analyzing Resource Availability  
- Only one failure_marker_alpha and one loadout_option_alpha are available, despite needing to restore four participants, raising concerns about resource sufficiency.  
- Bind actions release backup and failure marker resources, making them reusable, which should allow sequential processing of participants.  

## Considering Shared Roles  
- Primary_role_1 and secondary_role_1 are shared between coordinator_alpha and coordinator_beta, complicating readiness processes.  
- Making roles ready requires steps like probing, validating routes (e.g., route_segment_1), and initializing bundles, with shared resources like objective_node_1 affecting the process.

## Checking Resource Limitations  
- Only special_asset_alpha is initially available, while special_asset_beta, linked to coordinator_beta, is not, limiting certain actions.  
- Actions like lock_special_asset_on_coordinator require available special assets, so coordinator_beta's readiness may need alternative paths.  

## Exploring Role Readiness  
- Role_ready for primary_role_1 and secondary_role_1 can be achieved via mark_primary_ready and mark_secondary_ready, requiring steps like route validation and bundle initialization.  
- For coordinators, role_ready can be set using actions like finalize_modifier_and_mark_ready or finalize_asset_lock, but may depend on shared validated routes from primary and secondary roles.

## Examining Coordinator Integration  
- Finalize_fragment_integration sets coordinator_module_attached for coordinators like alpha, requiring conditions like committed status, has_fragment, and loadout_assigned, but not bundle_flag_route_tagged.  
- Integrate_progress_fragment first consumes the fragment, making it in_bundle, with progress_fragment_alpha available for coordinator_alpha and checkpoint_bundle_alpha.  

## Sequencing Coordinator Readiness  
- Attach_equipment_module and support_unit to coordinator_alpha, using available equipment_module_alpha and support_unit_alpha, sets has_equipment and support_attached, requiring committed status.  
- Sequence_mission_modifier_step1, with module_ready and has_mission_modifier, starts the sequence, leading to finalize_modifier_and_mark_ready setting coordinator_ready and role_ready for coordinator_alpha.

## Assessing Coordinator Beta's Options  
- Coordinator_beta lacks equipment_module_alpha and mission_modifier_alpha, already attached to coordinator_alpha, limiting direct replication.  
- Special_asset_beta is unavailable, restricting the special asset path for coordinator_beta's readiness.  

## Exploring Alternative Readiness Paths  
- Finalize_coordinator_readiness requires modifier_sequence_started and applied, but beta lacks mission_modifier, creating a blockage.  
- Tactical attachment via integrate_attachment_and_prepare_coordinator needs bundle_flag_route_tagged, which isn't standard for the initialized bundle.  

## Investigating Special Asset Strategy  
- The special asset path, using lock_special_asset_on_coordinator, doesn't require bundle validation, offering a potential route.  
- For coordinator_alpha, special_asset_alpha is available, but coordinator_beta still faces issues with unavailable special_asset_beta.

## Investigating Bundle Validation  
- Fragment integration requires bundle validation, but assembling a checkpoint bundle with route tags needs route_tagged, primary_probe_flag, and secondary validated conditions.  
- Applying consumable to primary segment sets route_tagged and primary_probe_flag, enabling assembly, even if primary isn't fully validated yet.  

## Assessing Validation Flexibility  
- Validate_bundle can occur without primary validation, allowing progress despite unvalidated primaries, with effects like setting probed and validated states.  
- Finalize_primary_segment_traversal validates primary after bundle validation, reusing consumable and ensuring readiness, but limited by single consumable availability.  

## Exploring Coordinator-Specific Paths  
- Two fragments available suggest potential for different readiness paths, but equipment and mission modifiers are singular, limiting flexibility.  
- No way to achieve module_ready without equipment phase, as coordinator_module_ready relies on equipment application actions.

## Evaluating Resource Sharing for Coordinators  
- Detach_mission_modifier and detach_equipment make resources like mission_modifier_alpha and equipment_module_alpha reusable, allowing sharing between coordinators.  
- Release_loadout_option and detach_support_unit similarly enable recycling single resources, but coordination is needed to manage limited availability.  

## Managing Backup Constraints  
- Only three backups (a, b, c) are available for four participants, requiring sequential assignment to avoid conflicts.  
- Backup_assigned is necessary before loadout commitment, as actions like propose_loadout_for_recovery require backup_claimed, limiting parallel readiness processes.  

## Planning Sequential Readiness  
- Process participants one by one: assign backups to three, complete their readiness, restore and release one backup, then assign to the fourth.  
- Restoring a backup requires the participant to be ready and marked, but early assignment is needed before starting loadout for recovery, complicating the order.

## Continuing Backup Restoration Process  
- After restoring primary, backups (a, b, c) are assigned to secondary, alpha, and beta, respectively, all claimed for the remaining three participants.  
- Attach failure markers and bind backups sequentially for secondary, alpha, and beta, restoring each while making backups and markers available again.  

## Managing Resource Reusability  
- Detach equipment_module_alpha, mission_modifier_alpha, and support_unit_alpha from alpha to make them available for beta’s readiness process.  
- Propose and commit loadouts for beta using the now-available resources, ensuring coordination despite limited equipment and modifiers.

## Preparing Coordinator Alpha  
- Attach mission modifier and sequence steps for coordinator alpha using checkpoint bundle alpha.  
- Finalize and mark coordinator alpha as ready after completing necessary integration steps.  

## Releasing Resources from Alpha  
- Detach support unit, equipment module, and mission modifier from coordinator alpha to free resources.  
- Release loadout option for coordinator alpha to make it available for other participants.  

## Restoring Primary Participant  
- Propose loadout for recovery and attach failure marker to primary role, using available loadout option alpha.  
- Bind backup to primary and resume, making backup and marker available again after restoration.

pddl2nl_prompt.note: autoplanbench_pddl2nl: run_save_descriptions.py --type full — шаблоны/JSON, без вызова LLM на построение промпта для сэмпла.

domain_setup.note: Файл domain_llm_usage_seedN.json не найден для section/subsection этого id (старый autoplanbench или домен не пересобирали). После run_domain_setup с обновлённым autoplanbench файл появится рядом с domain_description_seedN.json.

pipeline_usage.totals_scope_note: Per-run totals = planning + pddl2nl_prompt + nl2pddl (только tracked=true). domain_setup всегда вне totals (included_in_per_run_totals=false); цифры в блоке — для справки, агрегировать отдельно: domain_llm_usage_seed*.json + дедуп в collect_pipeline_token_usage.py.
